Joseph EL HADDAD

Brief of a story

joseph.ergo@proton.me | Portfolio | Resume PDF | Linked-In | +212 713-617-633

Rabat, Morocco (Open to Remote Worldwide)

Story resume

Pre programming era

Without making this document grim… I would only say that I lived with a tyrannical father.
At the age of 14, I asked for a new PC— ever since, I was forced to work with builders every summer, until I was 18.
I failed at university, both for the lack of French and transport money.
It was then when I decided to learn a skill that only require a computer.

Eye opening experience

The first thing I started leaning alone was web development, but only HTML and CSS— I said only, but later it proves useful when I started doing data scraping.

I couldn't for the life of me understand JavaScript, so ditched it… I did make a restaurant menu android app using HTML, CSS, and IONIC— but it was only for show.
In brief, I made it because in summer, I worked with builders in a restaurant, and I thought that making an attempt at digitizing their menu will make them give me a chance… but nope.

Then I watched a Ted Talk that changed my interest.
The best stats you've ever seen | Hans Rosling
This talk showed me how data is crucial to understand the world we live in— and this is how I started my data journey.

The hustle

I went from someone afraid of JavaScript to a someone who can use Python to manipulate and analyse data, and making predictive machine learning models.

No one wanted to give me a chance at a job because I don't have a diploma, so I decided to make an attempt at entrepreneurship.

In early 2019, I decided to add basic accounting and marketing to my skill roaster; then I made a LTD in the UK to attempt drop-shipping model.
Here is a prof of the LTD: https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/12080233/officers
COVID happened, War happened, and my gamble of making my own way failed.

My search

A while after living in anxiety and envy, I learned web scraping, attempted scraping data from Google map and some extra sites, sent few emails and got fewer encouraging responses.
I also made a resume video just to be unique and honest to self.
Plus, I learned Emacs and the value of taking notes, especially for someone with ADHD.

First major respond

The first was a marketing agency in NSW, they expressed their interesting, but they didn't know where do I fit.
They asked me how to target people working in NSW government so they can get more government related projects…
I had no idea myself, so I learned and attempted OSINT on an official, and this was the report I sent in two days— not much, but just because i don't know something doesn't mean I won't try to help.
Here is the essay:
https://josephelhaddad.github.io/20240401T034156--nsw-project__project.html

Second major respond

The second respond-er got intrigued by how much I packed in an email
Below is an example of said email.

While working remotely, I was in charge of a list collection project, as well as doing web research—All projects were end-to-end.
Plus, I was making bi-weekly reports using Canva and present them in a Zoom call.
Some of unique things I did without having asked to do them, except the last one:

  • Statistical summary of Google My Business Posts.
    For each industry, I analyzed the Emojis and Call to Actions used, here is a Google sheet.
    https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NAwFaRVqHOFTzRoFaqFKq2Y3dbp-ttLHZkbW9zND2uw/edit?gid=1813388989#gid=1813388989
  • Measuring business priority by FAV icon dimensions.
    I was wondering who to prioritize in the reach phase, then I got this idea.
    If a business have previously bought an expensive service, it means they are more likely to buy ours.
    A high quality FAV icon is a sign that the previous web developers are good at their job, thus likely expensive.
    Here is a Canva presentation, the stats are in page 3. (I only use Canva as thought organizer, I move the page, and say things that aren't preset in the page)
    https://www.canva.com/design/DAGYu0txri8/pI1cF4_Pp9IGOttpj9kDSQ/view#3
  • list of veterans only for east of communication
    The CEO was a Veteran, so I listed all business he need that have declared their owner as Veteran.
    https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1SvIfiEr2B9dk4gUQ7O05A9bcekCYpJsr/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=100716228614230911582&rtpof=true&sd=true
  • New listing app database
    Lastly, I was asked to do gather data to we can make a listing web app, I gathered as much as I could, and even provided a plan on how elements would be layer out.
    https://www.canva.com/design/DAGZS1PoaUw/sN4Al6XKYZk8EbHED-SaFA/view#1

During my research, I discovered a problem in Google manp and offered to share the story, so the business will get recognized, sadly it was deemed unworthy… so I shared it myself years later.
https://josephelhaddad.github.io/20250109T202901--google-map-plan__note.html

Present chapter

I attempted to start a project with a friend—I did my part that included acquiring media resources from YouTube videos using pure programming and AI to categorize media. Yet when it was hit turn to do his part, he procrastinate.

Months past, more than half my money I gathered was collected by my father.
I discover that my mother was in need of an operation last year, she asked her relatives from her side to take care of me and help me find a job if I came crashing.
I did, everyone left the house, yet I revived no help, nothing but blame.

So I start collecting data from Google map and a business relative website, but this time, I prioritized businesses that have a whats-app link for ease of communication.
All in the hope of finding a job that pays me enough to get a roof on top of my head and avoid all family nagging.

My apologies if all I talked about is myself and not how would I be of use to your endeavours.
I didn't get any traditional education because of my situation, I do need some on-boarding, but it is my way of learning and exploration what makes me good at finding solutions that other might not see.

Extra

I haven't find a place to talk about my learning.
I am a believer that education shouldn't end when you get a degree, mainly because I don't have one, but also because of my weird ADHD.
I even have a course on how to analyze your blood test report to determine what you need to improve your health.

Through it all, I might not be of use right away, but I know that I am worth being invested in.
Plus, I can start cheap.

P.S.:
Shortly after you contacted me, I had to say goodbye to my mother. thought she was sick, she went to another city to look for a job.
This chance mean a lot to me.
I hope I didn't make the document grim at the end.
